semantic_intent: Erosional coastline retreat creating isolated rock pillars and arches. Tidal forces carved horizontal bedding planes into flat shelves while vertical fractures formed sea stacks. Pearling noise simulates salt weathering on exposed faces.

Gradient.Scale.reason: Gentle gradient simulates tidal zone before wave action begins

Adjust.Mode.reason: Mimics wave-cut platform at sea level - flat intertidal zone

Slope Blur.Noise.reason: Salt crystallization pitting on vertical rock faces

Blur.Strength.reason: Tidal polish smoothing between high and low water marks

Erosion.Duration.reason: Light tidal erosion maintains stack stability over geological time

semantic_intent: Orogenic mountain belt with multiple peaks of varying age and uplift direction. Combines structural geology with differential erosion to create realistic alpine complexity.

Mountain.Range.Count.reason: Multiple orogenic pulses create realistic peak distribution

Transform.Scale.reason: Varying peak heights simulate differential uplift rates

Combine.Mode.reason: Natural overlap of alluvial fans at mountain base

Erosion.Duration.reason: Sufficient time for glacial carving and fluvial incision

semantic_intent: Caprock erosion where harder sandstone caps softer underlying strata. The flat top preserves the original plateau surface while vertical cliffs retreat through mechanical weathering and niche erosion.

Soft Clip.Max.reason: Mimics erosion-resistant caprock layer at mesa top

Curve.PointCount.reason: Creates stepped cliff profile matching sedimentary bedding planes

Adjust.Strength.reason: Maintains mesa height above surrounding pediment

Peneplain terrain approaching base level. Long-term erosion has subdued relief creating undulating topography. Periglacial processes may have contributed to smooth hillcrests. Atmospheric perspective in distance emphasizes silhouette.

Perlin.Scale.reason: Long wavelength captures regional geological structure

Perlin.Octaves.reason: Multiple frequency superposition mimics geological bedding

Blur.Strength.reason: Soil creep and weathering rounds interfluves

semantic_intent: High-altitude cirques and aretes where frost wedging creates steep faces while sheltered pockets accumulate snow. Aspect drives snow distribution - north faces retain longer. Erosion channels form natural avalanche paths.

Mountain.Height.reason: Treeline elevation creates dramatic alpine zone

Mountain.Seed.reason: Iterate for ridge-crest complexity and cirque formation

Erosion.Downcutting.reason: Glacial valley carving creates dramatic relief

Snow.Amount.reason: Heavy snow based on orographic precipitation patterns

semantic_intent: Composite volcanic cone with central crater. Radial symmetry with lava flow channels, ash deposits on slopes, and collapse caldera structure. Warp adds rheological flow distortion typical of viscous magma.

RadialGradient.Height.reason: Moderate height typical of stratovolcano profiles

Warp.Size.reason: Small warp mimics viscous lava flow deformation

Warp.Strength.reason: Lateral flow during cooling creates buttressing

Clip.Max.reason: Caldera collapse creating crater lake basin

semantic_intent: Dendritic drainage pattern carving through uplifted terrain. Long-profile equilibrium where rivers cut down through crustal uplift, creating characteristic V-shaped valleys with floodplains.

MountainRange.Height.reason: Sufficient elevation for gravitational potential in drainage

MountainRange.Scale.reason: Peak spacing controls drainage divide density

Erosion.Duration.reason: Long exposure creates mature valley network

Erosion.Detail.reason: Retains master stream characteristics over tributaries

Combine.Mode.reason: Blend effect only in masked areas

Deep river-cut canyon with entrenched meanders. Vertical strata exposure showing geological layers. The river has cut down through multiple formation levels creating exposed cliff faces of varying hardness.

Canyon.Depth.reason: Deep incision exposing multiple stratigraphic layers

Canyon.Width.reason: Narrow inner gorge with wider valley above

Erosion.Duration.reason: Long erosion exposure for mature canyon profile

semantic_intent: Fluvial delta where river meets standing water. Sediment fan spreading outward with distributary channels branching across the delta surface. Progradation builds land into the basin.

Erosion.Sediment.reason: High sediment load building the delta

semantic_intent: U-shaped glacial valley with hanging tributaries. Classic glacial carving where ice scraped out a deep trough with characteristic parabolic profile. Tributary valleys hang above the main valley.

Glacial.Depth.reason: Deep ice carving creates dramatic valley

semantic_intent: Dissolved limestone terrain with sinkholes and caves. Chemical weathering by acidic rainwater creates characteristic karst topography. Limestone pavements, grikes, and clints form where bedrock is exposed.
source_video_id: Master of Gaea - Creating Eroded Hoodoos Formations

semantic_intent: Erosional badlands with intricate drainage networks. Sparse vegetation allows rapid erosion creating maze-like valleys. Sharp divides between heavily eroded and intact areas.

Thermal.Strength.reason: Steep slopes fail through thermal cycling

semantic_intent: Glacial fjord carving where ice extended to sea level. Deep U-shaped valleys flooded by rising sea level. Steep walls, hanging valleys, and terminal moraines at the fjord mouth.

Glacial.Depth.reason: Deep glacial carving below sea level

Clip.Max.reason: Sea level intersection creating fjord floor

semantic_intent: Tectonically folded mountain belt with exposed geological strata. Anticlines and synclines visible in ridge and valley pattern. Erosion has differentially worn away layers to expose the folding.

Fold.Frequency.reason: Multiple fold wavelengths in the belt

semantic_intent: Coral atoll formation on submerged volcanic pedestal. Ring-shaped reef enclosing lagoon, with passes connecting to open ocean. Reef crest at sea level with slope dropping away.
source_video_id: Create Dynamic Sea Stacks in UE5.7

semantic_intent: Isolated flat-topped mesas and buttes in desert plateau. Caprock protection where harder sandstone preserves the top while softer underlying strata erode away.culverts form as notches in the cliff face.
Isolated rocky hills rising from flat plain. Resistant bedrock remnants standing above eroded surrounding terrain. Classic inselberg landscape where erosion has removed all softer material.
Steep volcanic island with radiating ridges and valleys. Tropical weathering creating dense vegetation patterns. River systems short and steep due to small catchment area.
Anastomosing river system with multiple interconnected channels. Stable channels with high sediment load creating islands and wetlands between braids. Common in areas with abundant fine sediment.
Glacial outwash plain where meltwaters deposited sediment. Flat terrain with braided stream patterns radiating from glacier terminus. Sorted sediments with decreasing grain size away from ice.
Arctic permafrost terrain with ice-cored pingos. Circular domes formed by ice lens injection. Thermokarst lakes in collapsed areas where ice has melted.
Sea cliff carved by wave action. Vertical cliff face with notches at varying heights from tidal range. Wave-cut platform at base exposed at low tide.
Conical alluvial fan where mountain stream exits to basin. Coarse sediment near apex, fine distal deposits. Channel migrates across fan surface during flood events.
Plateau being dissected by headward erosion. Dendritic drainage carving back into the plateau surface. Isolated remnants of the original plateau surface remain.
Eroded sandstone spires and chimneys. Vertical rock pillars formed where harder strata cap softer layers. Differential erosion leaves isolated towers.
source_video_id: Master of Gaea - Creating Eroded Hoodoos Formations
